deckedBuilder.targetMilestone: Example milestone: ship the next major version before a large convention or campaign window.
notes: This public repo uses illustrative sample numbers instead of private founder data.
Use the Imports screen to bring in your own App Store Connect exports.
Treat recurring software revenue as runway support first, then model upside separately.

funding.summary: Best current launch shape: founder-led, scenario-based, and disciplined on occupancy cost.
notes: Protect runway before chasing more space or more inventory.
Treat liquidation and outside support as scenario levers, not guaranteed facts.
Model software cash flow as support, not as a substitute for launch reserves.

siteCandidates.name: Neighborhood Strip Center
siteCandidates.summary: Balanced option with manageable rent and clear access.

siteCandidates.name: Main Corridor Site
siteCandidates.summary: Higher visibility, but potentially more expensive to carry.

siteCandidates.name: Convenience-Focused Option
siteCandidates.summary: Close to home or community base, with narrower growth ceiling.

scenarios.name: Lean
scenarios.summary: Viable if the site stays practical and reserves stay disciplined.

scenarios.name: Base
scenarios.summary: Enough breathing room for a healthy founder-led launch.

scenarios.name: Cushioned
scenarios.summary: Additional safety should strengthen runway, not rent tolerance.

sources.title: Bundled Sample Snapshot
sources.detail: The public repo ships with neutral sample numbers so the UI can preview cleanly.

sources.title: Imported Reports
sources.detail: Your real App Store Connect exports are imported locally at runtime and are not committed to the repo.

sources.title: Future API Integration
sources.detail: A later version can layer App Store Connect API sync on top of the current CSV and report import flow.
